title: Github Planning Workflow Observability P2

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Medium-priority planning workflow issues are accepted but should remain behind immediate trust and recoverability failures; when promoted, success should be measured by fewer raw-file reads and fewer human corrections, not by adding more workflow prose.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Define the smallest next-safe-action or intent-verification packet that can say answer-directly/no-artifact, use state.toml, or create an execplan, with tests for routine triage and durable intake.
proof_expectations: Fill in the narrowest command that proves the promoted work.
touched_scope: Fill in the concrete files before implementation starts.
completion_criteria: Intent verification, Memory consultation, proof selection, SkillSpec gates, operating-loop slots, and next-safe-action packets reduce live context by answering what to read, what to skip, what proof is required, and whether an artifact is warranted.

goal: Medium-priority planning workflow issues are accepted but should remain behind immediate trust and recoverability failures; when promoted, success should be measured by fewer raw-file reads and fewer human corrections, not by adding more workflow prose.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
intent.latitude: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.proof: Fill in the narrowest command that proves the promoted work.

touched: Fill in the concrete files before implementation starts.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Medium-priority planning workflow issues are accepted but should remain behind immediate trust and recoverability failures; when promoted, success should be measured by fewer raw-file reads and fewer human corrections, not by adding more workflow prose.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Medium-priority planning workflow issues are accepted but should remain behind immediate trust and recoverability failures; when promoted, success should be measured by fewer raw-file reads and fewer human corrections, not by adding more workflow prose.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Define the smallest next-safe-action or intent-verification packet that can say answer-directly/no-artifact, use state.toml, or create an execplan, with tests for routine triage and durable intake.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Fill in the concrete write scope before implementation starts.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Fill in an expected upper bound before implementation starts.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Fill in the narrowest command that proves the promoted work.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Define the smallest next-safe-action or intent-verification packet that can say answer-directly/no-artifact, use state.toml, or create an execplan, with tests for routine triage and durable intake.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Medium-priority planning workflow issues are accepted but should remain behind immediate trust and recoverability failures; when promoted, success should be measured by fewer raw-file reads and fewer human corrections, not by adding more workflow prose.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Medium-priority planning workflow issues are accepted but should remain behind immediate trust and recoverability failures; when promoted, success should be measured by fewer raw-file reads and fewer human corrections, not by adding more workflow prose.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.

drift_log: 2026-05-16: Promoted from TODO direct-task shape into an execplan.
